LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RICHARD TAVERNETTI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANT UNNECESSARILY IMPLANTED THE HIP PROSTHESIS FOR FINANCIAL GAIN BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAD A FINANCIAL INTEREST IN THE MANUFACTURER OF THE PROSTHESIS IS DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE MANNER IN WHICH PROFESSIONAL SERVICES WERE PROVIDED. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF MUST COMPLY WITH CCP 425.13 TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Susie Injijian, Attorney at Law, Tel. 510.898.1478, for plaintiffs;
Thomas M. Frieder, of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, for defendants Exactech, Inc.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT EXACTECH, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The Fourth Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately alleges that Dr. Tavernetti was acting as Exactech's agent at the time the alleged misrepresentations and/or concealments were made. The FAC pleads specific facts as to how the agency relationship was created and pleads specific facts showing how Exactech exerted control over Dr. Tavernetti at the time the alleged misrepresentations and/or concealments were made. (FAC, pars. 16-16, 77, 88.) Further, the court must accept as true allegations of an agency relationship for purposes of demurrer. (City of Industry v. City of Fillmore (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 191, 212-13.) The FAC also sufficiently pleads all of the elements of fraudulent misrepresentation: (1) Dr. Tavernetti misrepresented that P's only option was hip replacement (FAC, par. 71-72) (2) Dr. Tavernetti knew this misrepresentation was false (FAC, par. 72); (3) Dr. Tavernetti made this misrepresentation to induce P into getting an unnecessary surgery (FAC, par. 72); (4) P reasonably relied on these misrepresentations (FAC, par. 78); and (5) P was damaged as a result of these misrepresentations (FAC, pars. 80-81.) The FAC also sufficiently pleads all of the elements of fraudulent concealment: (1) Dr. Tavernetti concealed his relationship with Exactech (FAC, par. 86), (2) Plaintiff did not know about the concealed facts (FAC, par. 89); (3) Dr. Tavernetti intended to deceive Plaintiff by concealing these facts (FAC, pars. 88-87); (4) Plaintiff would have acted differently had the facts been disclosed (FAC, pars. 89-90); and (5) Plaintiff was harmed and the concealment was a substantial factor in her harm (FAC, pars. 91-92.). Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS M. FRIEDER OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EXACTECH, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE EIGHT AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION - Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Third Amended Complaint does not allege with the requisite particularity that Dr. Tavernetti was acting as Exactech's agent at the time the misrepresentations/concealments were made. The general allegations of agency alleged in paragraph 2 are insufficient, as fraud must be pled with particularity. The Third Amended Complaint also does not state facts establishing that Exactech ratified or authorized Dr. Tavernetti's statements. No facts are pled establishing that Exactech knew about the statements that Dr. Tavernetti made to Plaintiff. Although Plaintiff has been given multiple opportunities to amend, leave to amend is granted because there is a "reasonable possibility that the defect can be cured by amendment." (Goodman v. Kennedy (1976) 18 Cal.3d 335, 349.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KENDRA PAPPAS OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFEDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EXACTECH, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The court overrules the demurrer to the 4th cause of action [strict liability-manufacturing defect]. Paragraphs 34-37 of the amended complaint allege that the manufacturing defect included "material and component degradation, which in turn'created physical problems within patient's body, such as metal debris'" and the product differed from defendant's design and specifications. These allegations are sufficient. The court should overrules the demurrer to the 5th cause of action [strict liability-failure to warn]. Paragraphs 42 and 45 of the amended complaint allege that defendant failed to warn about complications/risks for young and physically active persons. The court overrules the demurrers to the 6th cause of action [negligent failure to warn] and 7th cause of action [negligence-manufacturing defect] based on the above. The court sustain the demurrer to the 8th cause of action [br of the implied warranty] without leave to amend. "It is settled law in California that privity between the parties is a necessary element to recovery on a breach of an implied warranty of fitness for the buyer's use'" Evraets, 29 Cal.App.4th at 788. "Liability on implied warranty has long been recognized, irrespective of negligence, where goods are sold. But (a) this liability is contractual, and requires privity between the person damaged by a defective article and the person liable; (b) timely notice of breach of warranty must be given; and (c) warranties may be excluded by disclaimers in the contract of sale." 6 Witkin Summary Ch. 9, sec. 1428, p. 852. Plaintiff's remedy is to sue in tort and pursue the strict liability causes of action. The court sustains the demurrer to the 9th cause of action [fraud/concealment] with leave to amend. First, plaintiff should allege the c/a with more particularity. While defendant can be liable for Dr. Tavernetti's misrepresentations based on an agency and/or ratification theory, the cause of action is not alleged with the requisite particularity. For example, paragraph 72 of the amended complaint does not allege whether the misrepresentations were oral or in writing. Second, plaintiff should allege the intentional misrepresentation and concealment causes of actions as distinct counts. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg
